notes: Initial hand-curated catalog for hsapi Phase 2.
Prefer 2026-03 date-versioned endpoints where HubSpot publishes them.
Keep legacy v3/v4 endpoints where current HubSpot docs still expose those paths or where date-versioned docs are incomplete.
Mutating typed commands should require --yes; destructive schema operations require an extra danger flag.

surfaces.name: marketing.ctas.javascript_sdk
surfaces.scopeNotes: HubSpot documents CTAs as a catalog-only browser JavaScript SDK surface under window.HubSpotCallsToActions, not as a bearer-token REST endpoint. Do not model this as an hsapi command or HubSpot API request.

surfaces.name: automation.workflows.docs
surfaces.scopeNotes: Workflows are documented here because HubSpot's workflow and custom-action model spans several older and newer guides.

surfaces.name: automation.sequences.docs
surfaces.scopeNotes: Sequences keep this guide as a docs-only surface; the REST list/get/enroll/status paths are tracked as endpoint rows.

endpoints.name: auth.oauth.token
endpoints.scopeNotes: OAuth authorization-code exchange. Sends client_id, client_secret, code, grant_type, and redirect_uri as form URL-encoded body fields. Response token values are redacted unless --show-secrets is used.

endpoints.name: auth.oauth.refresh
endpoints.scopeNotes: OAuth refresh-token exchange. Response token values are redacted unless --show-secrets is used.

endpoints.name: auth.oauth.introspect
endpoints.scopeNotes: Returns token metadata, user, hub, scopes, and app identifiers. Token-like values are redacted unless --show-secrets is used.

endpoints.name: auth.oauth.revoke
endpoints.scopeNotes: Revokes an OAuth token. Requires --danger-revoke-token and --yes.

endpoints.name: forms.secure_submit
endpoints.scopeNotes: Authenticated api.hsforms.com endpoint. HubSpot docs say authentication is required but omit the auth header in the cURL example; the CLI only sends the selected portal bearer token to the fixed secure form submission host/path.

endpoints.name: subscriptions.set_status
endpoints.scopeNotes: Data privacy settings may require legalBasis and legalBasisExplanation.

endpoints.name: subscriptions.unsubscribe_all
endpoints.scopeNotes: Opts the contact out of all email subscription types for the account or selected brand.

endpoints.name: subscriptions.batch_unsubscribe_all
endpoints.scopeNotes: Opts multiple contacts out of all email subscription types for the account or selected brand.

endpoints.name: subscriptions.generate_links
endpoints.scopeNotes: Beta endpoint. Requires communication_preferences.read_write or both communication_preferences.read and communication_preferences.write. Returned URLs are contact-specific and should be shared only with that contact.

endpoints.name: files.search
endpoints.scopeNotes: Search does not return hidden or archived files.

endpoints.name: files.get
endpoints.scopeNotes: Fetching hidden files by ID may require files.ui_hidden.read.

endpoints.name: files.signed_url
endpoints.scopeNotes: Signed URLs can expose private file content temporarily. Share output carefully.

endpoints.name: files.upload
endpoints.scopeNotes: Multipart upload. Requires explicit access level and folder target.

endpoints.name: files.import_url
endpoints.scopeNotes: Asynchronous import can copy external content into HubSpot file manager.

endpoints.name: files.replace
endpoints.scopeNotes: Replaces file bytes while preserving the file ID and downstream references.

endpoints.name: files.delete
endpoints.scopeNotes: Marks file as deleted and makes content inaccessible.

endpoints.name: files.gdpr_delete
endpoints.scopeNotes: Permanently deletes file content and metadata within 7 days. CLI requires --danger-gdpr-delete plus --yes.

endpoints.name: files.folders.update_async
endpoints.scopeNotes: Async folder update can rename or move a folder and update child paths.

endpoints.name: events.types
endpoints.scopeNotes: Event API scopes vary by event family and associated CRM object. For occurrence reads, include the scopes for objects whose events are queried.

endpoints.name: events.occurrences
endpoints.scopeNotes: Event API scopes vary by event family and associated CRM object. For occurrence reads, include the scopes for objects whose events are queried.

endpoints.name: webhooks.settings
endpoints.scopeNotes: Classic app webhooks are app-level configuration endpoints. They may require developer app auth/API-key style access rather than a normal private-app portal token.

endpoints.name: webhooks.settings_update
endpoints.scopeNotes: Classic app webhooks are app-level configuration endpoints. They may require developer app auth/API-key style access rather than a normal private-app portal token.

endpoints.name: webhooks.settings_delete
endpoints.scopeNotes: Classic app webhooks are app-level configuration endpoints. They may require developer app auth/API-key style access rather than a normal private-app portal token.

endpoints.name: webhooks.subscription_create
endpoints.scopeNotes: Classic app webhooks are app-level configuration endpoints. They may require developer app auth/API-key style access rather than a normal private-app portal token.

endpoints.name: webhooks.subscription_update
endpoints.scopeNotes: Classic app webhooks are app-level configuration endpoints. They may require developer app auth/API-key style access rather than a normal private-app portal token.

endpoints.name: webhooks.subscription_batch_update
endpoints.scopeNotes: Classic app webhooks are app-level configuration endpoints. They may require developer app auth/API-key style access rather than a normal private-app portal token.

endpoints.name: conversations.visitor_token
endpoints.scopeNotes: Visitor identification returns a token that identifies a website visitor. Treat token output as sensitive.

endpoints.name: forms.archive
endpoints.scopeNotes: Archives the form and stops new submissions; HubSpot permanently deletes the definition after roughly three months.

endpoints.name: forms.submissions
endpoints.scopeNotes: Docs allow forms or forms-uploaded-files scope. Output includes submitted field values and page URLs.

endpoints.name: forms.submit
endpoints.scopeNotes: Unauthenticated api.hsforms.com endpoint. The CLI sends no bearer token for this command.

endpoints.name: marketing.emails.create
endpoints.scopeNotes: Marketing emails are separate from CRM sales emails and content-impacting writes should be previewed before execution.

endpoints.name: marketing.transactional.send
endpoints.scopeNotes: Transactional email is separate from marketing email and requires the dedicated transactional-email capability.

endpoints.name: cms.hubdb.tables.list
endpoints.scopeNotes: Published tables can be public when HubDB allows public API access and portalId is provided.

endpoints.name: cms.hubdb.tables.get
endpoints.scopeNotes: Treat HubDB table metadata as content-sensitive when the table feeds live pages or programmable emails.

endpoints.name: cms.hubdb.tables.create
endpoints.scopeNotes: Creates a draft HubDB table. Draft/live changes can affect dynamic pages and programmable email data sources.

endpoints.name: cms.hubdb.rows.create
endpoints.scopeNotes: Adding rows can change live CMS rendering when the table powers pages or programmable email content.

endpoints.name: cms.source_code.upload
endpoints.scopeNotes: Uploads template/module/CSS/JS assets to the HubSpot developer file system.

endpoints.name: cms.source_code.validate
endpoints.scopeNotes: Validation can surface HubL and CMS asset errors before a file is published.

endpoints.name: cms.source_code.delete
endpoints.scopeNotes: Deleting published source files can immediately affect live CMS assets.

endpoints.name: automation.workflows.list
endpoints.scopeNotes: Legacy v3 metadata-only workflow list surface. Workflow API access can depend on the portal's automation capabilities.

endpoints.name: automation.workflows.get
endpoints.scopeNotes: Can include validation errors or workflow statistics when requested with query parameters.

endpoints.name: automation.workflows.current_enrollment
endpoints.scopeNotes: Lists workflows a contact is currently enrolled in and can expose contact-specific automation state.

endpoints.name: automation.workflows.enroll_contact
endpoints.scopeNotes: Legacy workflow enrollment path with no request body. Confirm the contact email and workflow ID before using --yes.

endpoints.name: automation.sequences.list
endpoints.scopeNotes: Sequences require an appropriate Sales or Service seat. Treat the result as sequence metadata rather than CRM data.

endpoints.name: automation.sequences.get
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires the acting userId query parameter and an appropriate Sales or Service seat.

endpoints.name: automation.sequences.enroll_contact
endpoints.scopeNotes: Enrollments need `userId` plus contact, sequence, and sender details. Treat the request as live sales sequencing.

endpoints.name: automation.sequences.enrollment_status
endpoints.scopeNotes: Reads whether a contact is currently enrolled in a sequence; the status can expose sales follow-up context.

endpoints.name: crm.extensions.calling.settings.get
endpoints.scopeNotes: Reads app-level calling extension settings such as app URL, dimensions, readiness, and inbound/custom object support.

endpoints.name: crm.extensions.calling.settings.delete
endpoints.scopeNotes: Deletes app-level calling extension settings and cannot be undone. CLI requires --yes for live deletes.

endpoints.name: crm.extensions.calling.recording_settings.get
endpoints.scopeNotes: Returns the authenticated-recording callback URL HubSpot will use for call recording retrieval.

endpoints.name: crm.extensions.calling.recording_settings.create
endpoints.scopeNotes: Registers the endpoint HubSpot will call to retrieve authenticated call recordings. The endpoint URL must include `%s` for the externalId placeholder.

endpoints.name: crm.extensions.calling.recording_settings.update
endpoints.scopeNotes: Updates the recording callback URL HubSpot will use for authenticated recording retrieval.

endpoints.name: crm.extensions.calling.channel_connection.delete
endpoints.scopeNotes: Deleting the channel connection disconnects the calling app from the connected device setup. CLI requires --yes for live deletes.

endpoints.name: crm.extensions.calling.recordings.ready
endpoints.scopeNotes: Notifies HubSpot that an external recording is ready for transcription.

endpoints.name: crm.extensions.calling.transcripts.create
endpoints.scopeNotes: Creates an integrator-provided transcript for a call engagement using transcript utterances.

endpoints.name: crm.extensions.calling.transcripts.get
endpoints.scopeNotes: Reads HubSpot-generated or integrator-generated transcript text, timing, and speaker metadata for a call engagement.

endpoints.name: crm.extensions.videoconferencing.settings.get
endpoints.scopeNotes: Reads app-level video conferencing extension settings for the configured app integration.

endpoints.name: crm.extensions.videoconferencing.settings.delete
endpoints.scopeNotes: Deletes app-level video conferencing extension settings and cannot be undone. CLI requires --yes for live deletes.

endpoints.name: scheduler.calendar.create
endpoints.scopeNotes: Docs require OAuth authorization and organizerUserId query parameter.

endpoints.name: imports.start
endpoints.scopeNotes: Multipart upload. Import files can create/update many records, create lists, and affect marketing contact status.

endpoints.name: imports.errors
endpoints.scopeNotes: May include row values from the imported file when includeRowData is requested.

endpoints.name: exports.start
endpoints.scopeNotes: Exports can expose bulk CRM data and generate temporary unauthenticated download URLs.

endpoints.name: exports.status
endpoints.scopeNotes: Completed export status can include a temporary download URL that should be treated as sensitive.

endpoints.name: lists.membership_update
endpoints.scopeNotes: Only valid for MANUAL or SNAPSHOT lists. DYNAMIC lists are controlled by filters.

endpoints.name: objects.create
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires write scope for the selected CRM object type.

endpoints.name: objects.archive
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires write scope for the selected CRM object type. Archives the record into HubSpot's recycling bin.

endpoints.name: objects.merge
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires write scope for the selected CRM object type. Merges another record into the primary record and can archive the duplicate.

endpoints.name: objects.gdpr_delete
endpoints.scopeNotes: Permanent GDPR delete. Confirm object type and identifier before running.

endpoints.name: objects.batch_read
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires read scope for the selected CRM object type.

endpoints.name: objects.batch_create
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires write scope for the selected CRM object type.

endpoints.name: objects.batch_update
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires write scope for the selected CRM object type.

endpoints.name: objects.batch_upsert
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires write scope for the selected CRM object type. Upsert needs a unique property identifier on each input.

endpoints.name: objects.batch_archive
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires write scope for the selected CRM object type. Archives records by ID.

endpoints.name: associations.records.create_default
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires write access to the selected CRM object relationship. Default associations do not add a custom label.

endpoints.name: associations.records.create_labeled
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires the correct directional association type ID and write access for the selected object relationship.

endpoints.name: associations.records.delete
endpoints.scopeNotes: Removes all associations between the two records.

endpoints.name: associations.records.batch_read
endpoints.scopeNotes: Batch read accepts up to 1,000 source record IDs per request body.

endpoints.name: associations.records.batch_create_default
endpoints.scopeNotes: Creates unlabeled/default associations in bulk.

endpoints.name: associations.records.batch_create_labeled
endpoints.scopeNotes: Creates labeled associations in bulk. Use the directional associationTypeId for the from/to direction.

endpoints.name: associations.records.batch_archive
endpoints.scopeNotes: Removes all associations between each from/to pair in the request body.

endpoints.name: associations.records.batch_labels_archive
endpoints.scopeNotes: Removes specific labels from associations while leaving other labels or the unlabeled association intact when present.

endpoints.name: properties.create
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires the matching CRM schema/property write scope for the object type. Scope names vary by object family and app type.

endpoints.name: properties.update
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires the matching CRM schema/property write scope for the object type. Be careful with calculated or UI-created rollup properties.

endpoints.name: properties.archive
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires the matching CRM schema/property write scope for the object type. Built-in properties and properties in active use may not be archivable.

endpoints.name: pipeline_stages.create
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires pipeline write access for the object type. Deal stages require probability metadata.

endpoints.name: pipeline_stages.update
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires pipeline write access for the object type. Moving or changing stages can affect reports, automations, and record routing.

endpoints.name: pipeline_stages.delete
endpoints.scopeNotes: Requires pipeline write access for the object type. Deleting stages can break records, workflows, reporting, and forecasts if not prepared first.
